questions.question: Compare static and dynamic scheduling in OpenMP, highlighting two advantages of each.
expectedPoints: Static: Lower runtime overhead, better data locality
Dynamic: Better load balancing for irregular workloads, adaptable to varying iteration times

questions.question: Explain how task dependencies work in OpenMP and provide an example where they would be beneficial.
expectedPoints: Runtime creates dependency graph between tasks
Enables correct execution ordering without explicit synchronization
Example: Pipeline processing where each stage depends on previous stage output
Better than barriers for fine-grained synchronization
